reasoning_model_1: Model 1 perfectly follows the content instructions with no errors in the script. The audio content matches the text '我相信自己能胜任这份工作，我会全力以赴。' exactly. In terms of the paralinguistic feature 'confidence' (认知状态), the speaker delivers the lines with a steady, firm, and self-assured tone throughout (0.0s - 2.8s). The pacing is natural, and the stress is placed appropriately on '相信' (believe) and '全力以赴' (go all out) to convey determination without sounding aggressive. It meets the criteria for a high-quality, confident generation.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates a high level of energy and distinct confidence in its tone, fitting the 'cognitive state' requirement well in terms of expressiveness. However, it fails significantly on Content Accuracy. The model hallucinates and adds an entire phrase '不负期待' (won't let down expectations) at the end (approx 2.7s - 3.5s), which was not in the source text. While the core script is present, the addition of unrequested text violates the instruction to read the specific sentence. The prosody is also slightly exaggerated compared to a natural confident speaking style, but the text error is the primary issue.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It strictly adheres to the content constraints while successfully conveying the required confident tone in a natural, professional manner. Model 2, while expressive, fails the fundamental requirement of Content Accuracy by adding the phrase '不负期待' to the end of the script. In a strict evaluation of controlled audio generation, adhering to the provided text is a prerequisite before evaluating the quality of the emotional rendering. Therefore, Model 1 is superior due to its precision.
